babyDevelopment: The baby's eyes are starting to make slow side-to-side movements. Ears are nearing their final position on the head. The baby can now perform more complex movements, like grasping and squinting. The circulatory system is becoming more advanced.
bodyChanges: You may start to feel early fetal movements, often described as 'flutters' or 'quickening,' especially if this isn't your first pregnancy. Your uterus is about halfway between your pubic bone and your navel.
tips: Schedule your mid-pregnancy anatomy scan (usually between weeks 18-22)
Include more iron-rich foods to prevent anemia
Practice sleeping on your side if you haven't yet

do: Keep up with your walking or swimming routine
Stay hydrated (about 10 cups of fluid a day)
Focus on good spinal alignment when sitting
dont: Ignore any sharp, persistent pelvic pain

travelAdvisory.note: A comfortable time for travel before later discomfort begins.

warningSignsToWatch: Signs of a urinary tract infection
milestoneDescription: You might start feeling the baby's first fluttering movements.
